# registry_api_key (Resource)

Manages a registry API key. The raw key value is only available at creation time and stored in state.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "registry_api_key" "ci" {
  organization_id = registry_organization.example.id
  name            = "ci-pipeline"
  description     = "API key for CI/CD pipeline"
  scopes          = ["modules:read", "modules:write", "providers:read", "providers:write"]
  expires_at      = "2027-01-01T00:00:00Z"
}

output "ci_api_key" {
  value     = registry_api_key.ci.key
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Friendly name for the API key.
- `organization_id` (String) UUID of the organization this key belongs to.
- `scopes` (List of String) List of permission scopes (e.g., ['modules:read', 'modules:write']).

### Optional

- `description` (String) Optional description.
- `expires_at` (String) ISO 8601 expiration timestamp. Omit for no expiration.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) ISO 8601 timestamp when the key was created.
- `id` (String) UUID of the API key.
- `key` (String, Sensitive) The raw API key value. Only populated at creation time — store this securely.
- `key_prefix` (String) First few characters of the key, for identification.
- `last_used_at` (String) ISO 8601 timestamp of last key use.
